seo.title: Cabinet Refinishing in Spokane WA | Cabinets Plus
seo.description: Refresh your kitchen without the cost of replacement. Cabinets Plus offers cabinet refinishing, painting, refacing, and wood tone changes in Spokane & Spokane Valley — done in-house by a full cabinet shop.

blocks.heading: Cabinet Refinishing in Spokane WA
blocks.subtext: Don't replace — refinish. Give the cabinets you already have a completely new look — without the cost, mess, or weeks of downtime that come with a full replacement.

blocks.title: Refinishing, Built on a Foundation of Cabinet Expertise
blocks.paragraphs:
For years, our customers kept asking the same question: instead of replacing our cabinets, can you just refresh the ones we have? The demand was clear — so we built the service to meet it.

As a Spokane kitchen and cabinet company first, we already have everything refinishing requires under one roof: the shop space, the finishing equipment, the material sourcing, and a team that understands how cabinets are built, finished, and meant to hold up over time. That foundation lets us offer refinishing that goes well beyond a coat of paint.

Whether you want to modernize a dated kitchen, brighten the whole space, or preserve the character of cabinets you already love, our team can give them a fresh, lasting finish — handled in-house from start to finish, right here in Spokane Valley.

blocks.title: Why Refinish Instead of Replace
blocks.paragraphs:
Most refinishing projects wrap in days, not weeks. You get back to enjoying your kitchen faster, with less mess and less disruption than a full replacement.

Refinishing can also create a high-end, custom look for a fraction of the cost of replacing every cabinet. A refreshed kitchen boosts your home's appeal and resale value, making it a smart, low-risk investment when your existing cabinet boxes are still solid.

And because we're a full cabinet shop — not a paint crew — every project is backed by real cabinetry experience.

steps.title: Consultation & Assessment
steps.description: We start with a free consultation, talk through your goals, and recommend the right approach — paint, refinish, reface, or a combination. You'll leave with a clear plan and a no-pressure quote.

steps.title: Prep, Repair & Finish Selection
steps.description: We clean, sand, and properly prep every surface, handle any repairs, and help you lock in your color or wood tone so the final result looks intentional and lasts.

steps.title: Refinishing & Reveal
steps.description: Our team applies your finish with shop-grade equipment and attention to detail, then reinstalls everything clean. The result: cabinets that look brand new — in a fraction of the time a full remodel would take.

blocks.title: Finish & Color
blocks.paragraphs:
**Cabinet painting**, **cabinet refinishing**, and **wood tone refinishing** all refresh your existing cabinets without replacing a single box — the right choice comes down to the look you're after and the condition of your current finish. **Cabinet painting** lays down a smooth, solid color that covers the wood grain completely — the go-to for taking dated oak or honey-stained cabinets to a clean, modern white, navy, gray, or black. **Cabinet refinishing** strips the worn finish back and rebuilds it from the surface up, restoring a durable, like-new coat on the cabinets you already have. **Wood tone refinishing** re-stains your cabinets to a new shade — lighter, darker, warmer, or cooler — while letting the natural wood grain show through.

In short: paint to cover the wood, refinish to renew it, wood tone to recolor it.

blocks.title: Surface & Structure
blocks.paragraphs:
Sometimes the doors and drawer fronts are the problem, but your cabinet boxes are still structurally sound — and when that's the case, there's no reason to pay for a full replacement. **Cabinet door replacement** swaps out the existing doors and drawer fronts for an updated style and leaves your boxes in place. It's the most budget-friendly way to modernize a kitchen, and a great fit when the frames are solid but the fronts look tired or damaged. **Cabinet refacing** takes it a step further: new doors and drawer fronts plus a fresh hardwood skin over the visible box surfaces, so every part of the kitchen looks brand new — all while keeping your existing layout and footprint.

Choose door replacement if your boxes still look good; choose refacing for a complete visual reset without a full remodel.

blocks.title: Modifications & Upgrades
blocks.paragraphs:
Beyond color and surfaces, **cabinet modifications** and **hardware upgrades** make your existing cabinets work better and feel better every day. **Cabinet modifications & upgrades** cover the practical side: repairs, adjustments, and structural tweaks that adapt aging or awkward cabinetry to the way you actually use your kitchen — from fixing worn components to improving how doors and drawers open and close. **Hardware & soft-close upgrades** swap in modern pulls, knobs, and hinges and add soft-close mechanisms so doors and drawers shut quietly on their own. It's one of the smallest changes you can make, with one of the biggest payoffs in how high-end the kitchen feels.

Both pair naturally with any painting, refinishing, or refacing work for a complete, top-to-bottom refresh.

blocks.title: Add-Ons
blocks.paragraphs: Two optional add-ons — **interior door refinishing** and **window trim refinishing** — carry your refreshed look beyond the cabinets to the surrounding woodwork, so nothing in the room feels left behind. **Interior door refinishing** brings full-size interior doors in line with your newly finished cabinets, and **window trim refinishing** matches your window trim and packages to the same updated color or tone. Offered as part of a cabinet refinishing project, they tie the entire space together for a cohesive, finished result.

faqs.question: How long does cabinet refinishing take?
faqs.answer: Most projects are completed in a matter of days rather than the weeks a full replacement can take. Exact timing depends on the size of your kitchen and the services involved — we'll give you a clear timeline at your consultation.

faqs.question: Is a refinished cabinet as durable as a new one?
faqs.answer: Yes. Because we properly prep every surface and use shop-grade finishes, a refinished cabinet stands up to daily kitchen use just like a factory finish. The durability comes from the prep work — which is exactly where our cabinet experience pays off.

faqs.question: Can you change the color or wood tone of my cabinets?
faqs.answer: Absolutely. We can paint cabinets a new color, refinish them for a fresh surface, or change the wood tone entirely — lighter, darker, or a completely different look. If you can picture it, we can usually match it.

faqs.question: What's the difference between refinishing, refacing, and painting?
faqs.answer: Painting applies a solid color over your existing cabinets. Refinishing strips and rebuilds the finish for a renewed surface. Refacing replaces the doors and adds a new hardwood skin to the boxes for a brand-new look — all while keeping your existing layout. We'll help you pick the right one at your consultation.

faqs.question: Do I have to move out during the project?
faqs.answer: No. Refinishing is one of the least disruptive ways to update a kitchen. We work clean, keep our footprint contained, and stay out of your way as much as possible.

faqs.question: Should I refinish or replace my cabinets?
faqs.answer: If your cabinet boxes are structurally sound and you're mainly after a new look, refinishing gives you a high-end result for far less than replacement. If your layout isn't working or the cabinets are failing, replacement may make more sense — and we can walk you through both options honestly at your consultation.

faqs.question: Which cabinets and surfaces can be refinished?
faqs.answer: Most solid wood and quality cabinetry refinishes beautifully. During your free assessment, we'll confirm what's a good candidate and recommend the best approach for your specific cabinets.

blocks.heading: We can refresh the space you already love.
blocks.subtext: Get a free, no-pressure refinishing consultation with Spokane's cabinet experts. Give us a call — (509) 218-3349.
